title: Born in Ulm
description: Albert Einstein is born on March 14, 1879, in Ulm, Germany.

title: Family Moves to Munich
description: In 1880 the Einstein family moves to Munich.

title: Discovers the Compass Wonder
description: As a child in the 1880s, Einstein becomes fascinated by a compass shown to him by his father.

title: Leaves Munich for Italy
description: In 1894 Einstein leaves school in Munich and rejoins his family in Italy.

title: Studies in Aarau
description: After failing the Zurich Polytechnic entrance exam in 1895, he studies in Aarau to complete his schooling.

title: Enters ETH Zurich
description: In 1896 Einstein enters the Polytechnic in Zurich to study mathematics and physics.

title: Renounces German Citizenship
description: In 1896 Einstein gives up his German citizenship and remains stateless for several years.

title: Meets Michele Besso
description: In 1897 Einstein meets Michele Besso, who becomes a lifelong friend and intellectual sounding board.

title: Graduates from ETH
description: Einstein graduates from ETH Zurich in 1900.

title: Becomes Swiss Citizen
description: In 1901 Einstein becomes a Swiss citizen.

title: Joins Bern Patent Office
description: In June 1902 Einstein begins work at the Swiss patent office in Bern.

title: Marries Mileva Maric
description: Einstein marries Mileva Maric on January 6, 1903.

title: Annus Mirabilis Papers
description: In 1905 Einstein publishes groundbreaking papers on Brownian motion, special relativity, mass-energy equivalence, and the photoelectric effect.

title: Earns Doctorate
description: Einstein's doctoral work is accepted in 1905, and he receives the degree from the University of Zurich in 1906.

title: First Professorship in Zurich
description: In 1909 Einstein leaves the patent office to become a professor at the University of Zurich.

title: Professorship in Prague
description: In 1911 Einstein accepts a professorship at the German University in Prague.

title: Returns to ETH Zurich
description: In 1912 Einstein returns to Zurich to teach at ETH.

title: Moves to Berlin
description: In 1914 Einstein moves to Berlin to join the Prussian Academy of Sciences.

title: General Relativity
description: In 1915 Einstein presents the field equations of general relativity.

title: Eclipse Confirms Relativity
description: In 1919 eclipse observations help make Einstein world-famous by supporting general relativity's light-bending prediction.

title: Divorces and Marries Elsa
description: In 1919 Einstein divorces Mileva Maric and later marries Elsa Einstein.

title: Wins Nobel Prize
description: Einstein receives the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics, formally awarded in 1922, chiefly for the photoelectric effect.

title: Bose-Einstein Work
description: In 1924 Einstein develops key work with Satyendra Nath Bose on what becomes Bose-Einstein statistics and condensation.

title: Emigrates to the United States
description: After the Nazis seize power in 1933, Einstein settles in the United States and joins the Institute for Advanced Study.

title: Publishes EPR Paper
description: In 1935 Einstein coauthors the EPR paper challenging the completeness of quantum mechanics.

title: Letter to Roosevelt
description: Einstein signs the August 2, 1939, letter warning President Roosevelt about nuclear chain reactions and the danger of a bomb program.

title: Becomes U.S. Citizen
description: On October 1, 1940, Einstein becomes a citizen of the United States.

title: Declines Israel Presidency
description: In 1952 Einstein declines the offer to become president of Israel.

title: Dies in Princeton
description: Albert Einstein dies in Princeton on April 18, 1955.
